title: Telemetry Channel Definitions
description: Sensor definitions and physical quantity/unit metadata for the racetelem telemetry system. Source of truth for proto generation and DB migrations.

quantities.description: Physical measurement types with unit conversions. Sensors reference these by key.

sensors.description: Telemetry channel definitions. Keys become proto field names and DB column names. Naming convention: {system}_{measurement}[_{location}]

propertyNames.description: Sensor names must be lowercase snake_case, valid as both protobuf field names and SQL column names.

reserved_tags.description: Retired tag numbers that must NEVER be reused. Keys are tag numbers (as strings), values describe the original sensor and reason for retirement.

quantity.description: A physical measurement type (e.g., temperature, pressure, speed) with a base unit and optional display unit conversions.

description.description: Human-readable description of this physical quantity.

base_unit.description: Key of the base unit (must exist in 'units'). This is the unit stored on the wire and in the database.

units.description: Available units for this quantity. The base unit must be listed here (with only a symbol). Non-base units include scale and optional offset for conversion: display_value = base_value * scale + offset

unit.description: A unit of measurement. Base units need only a symbol. Non-base units require at least 'scale' for conversion: display_value = base_value * scale + offset

symbol.description: Display symbol for this unit (e.g., "°C", "kPa", "rpm"). Empty string for dimensionless.

scale.description: Multiplication factor to convert from base unit to this unit. Base units omit this (implicit 1.0).

offset.description: Additive offset applied after scaling: display = base * scale + offset. Default: 0.

sensor.description: A telemetry sensor/channel definition. The key becomes the proto field name and DB column name.

tag.description: Protobuf field number. IMMUTABLE once assigned — never change or reuse. Tags 1-2 are reserved for structural fields. Tags 3-15 get 1-byte encoding (use for highest-frequency sensors).

quantity.description: Reference to a key in the 'quantities' section. Determines the base unit and available display units.

proto_type.description: Protobuf field type. Maps to SQL: float→REAL, double→DOUBLE PRECISION, uint32/int32→INTEGER, int64→BIGINT, bytes→BYTEA.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this sensor measures.

group.description: Organizational group for UI grouping and proto file comments.

location.description: Spatial qualifier for per-corner or front/rear sensors.

min.description: Expected minimum value in base unit. For display range hints and validation.

max.description: Expected maximum value in base unit. For display range hints and validation.

precision.description: Decimal places for display (0 = integer). Default: 1.

sample_rate_hz.description: Typical sample rate in Hz. Informational only, not enforced.

can.description: Default CAN bus mapping for your own sensor hardware.

can_mapping.description: CAN bus signal mapping for a sensor.

id.description: CAN arbitration ID (use hex in YAML: 0x360).

start_bit.description: Start bit position in the CAN frame (0-63).

byte_order.description: Byte order of the CAN signal. Default: little_endian.
